channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: National nonprofit asks Gov. McMaster to 'intervene' in South Carolina
item.description: A controversial Texas-based nonprofit promising to set up citizen patrols in McColl is now asking for state help.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: The Speaker’s Lobby: Thermonuclear Fracas
item.description: Despite the intent to find solutions following the assassination attempt of President-elect Trump back in July, bipartisan lawmakers got into a heated exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The DNC chair candidates discuss Joe Biden, Kamala Harris and winning Latino voters
item.description: Four Democrats are running for Democratic National Committee chair, looking to take on the task of reinvigorating a party demoralized by a second loss to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mother of Austin Tice, journalist missing in Syria, says new information proves her son is alive
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for more than a decade, said Friday that she was confident her son was alive, citing information she said had come from a “significant source” that she did not identify but said had been vetted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis Mayor Frey poised to block council again as his veto use continues to climb
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed 15 actions since he first took office in 2018. That’s more vetoes than the previous two mayors combined.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps forceful ally of hard-line immigration policies to head Customs and Border Protection
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The picture of who will be in charge of executing President-elect Donald Trump's hard-line immigration and border policies has come into sharper focus after he announced his picks to head Customs and Border Protection and also the agency tasked with deporting immigrants in the coun
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On pardons, Biden weighs whether to flex presidential powers in broad new ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden faces a stark choice as he contemplates broad preemptive pardons to protect aides and allies from potential retribution by Donald Trump: Does he hew to the institutional norms he’s spent decades defending or flex the powers of the presidency in untested ways? Th
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Vance tells residents in hurricane-stricken North Carolina that they haven't been forgotten
item.description: FAIRVIEW, N.C. (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance on Friday assured residents of western North Carolina still cleaning up from Hurricane Helene that they haven't been forgotten as he surveyed storm wreckage and talked to first responders in one of his first public appearances since the election.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and GARY ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the billionaires stocking Trump’s next administration
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several billionaires to top roles in his next administration — a combination worth over $360 billion and that includes the world’s richest person.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The first rioter tried on Jan. 6 charges gets reduced prison sentence after Supreme Court decision
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Texas man who was the first rioter to go on trial for the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol was resentenced on Friday to nearly seven years in prison after he delivered an angry, profane rant to the judge who agreed to modestly reduce his original sentence.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boston City Council member is arrested on fraud and theft charges
item.description: Boston city councilor Tania Fernandes Anderson was arrested Friday on federal fraud charges, accused of funneling part of an inflated bonus payment to a staffer to whom she was related into her own pockets during an exchange at a City Hall bathroom, the U.S. attorney's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A court upholds the U.S. ban on TikTok
item.description: The appeals court agreed that the viral video app must be sold off by its China-based parent company, rejecting TikTok's claim that the crackdown violates the free speech rights of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: How measles, whooping cough and worse could roar back on RFK Jr.’s watch
item.description: With anti-vaccine activist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in charge of the nation’s biggest health agency, it wouldn’t take much tampering to enable vaccine-preventable diseases to make a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Arthur Allen, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Anthony Salisbury as deputy Homeland Security advisor
item.description: Anthony Salisbury is currently a special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) in Miami.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Head of Democratic Caucus says thin House margins make attendance vital next year
item.description: The head of the House Democratic Caucus is sending an early message to his troops heading into the 119th Congress: Attendance will be crucial given the razor-tight margins in the lower chamber. “It’s important for every member to come to work and to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho 'abortion trafficking' law will be enforced in part, court rules
item.description: Idaho can enforce a first-of-its-kind "abortion trafficking" law, a federal court ruled, making it illegal for anyone who transports a minor across state lines to get an abortion without parental consent.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are bringing Trump’s DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: The leaders of the so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” are calling for large-scale layoffs of federal workers and the elimination of some federal agencies during Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: “I am pleased to announce that former Congressman Billy Long, of the Great State of Missouri, will be appointed to serve as the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS),” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Kelly Loeffler for SBA
item.description: The former Georgia lawmaker, who served about a year in the Senate starting in January 2020, also has been overseeing the incoming president’s inaugural committee with Steve Witkoff.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Trump criticizes Chronister after sheriff's withdrawal, days after praising him
item.description: President Donald Trump challenged reports of Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister's DEA withdrawal, saying he didn't want the sheriff days his cabinet days after nominating him.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Trump team signs an agreement with the DOJ for security clearances
item.description: The new agreement will help Trump officials on agency landing teams access classified information needed to prepare to take over on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrats stick with Schumer as leader
item.description: Chuck Schumer faced no opposition in the party leadership elections, in which Illinois Sen. Dick Durbin was also re-elected to the No. 2 spot and Minnesota Sen. Amy Klobuchar became the new No. 3.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. will send Ukraine $725 million more in arms
item.description: President Joe Biden has pledged to spend all of the military assistance funds Congress approved this year for Ukraine before the end of his administration on Jan. 20, 2025.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to nominate Kash Patel to serve as head of the FBI, elevating a staunch ally and controversial figure who sparked internal battles during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC
